Latest 10-K business section (newest first):
  filing_date=2023-03-08 section=business period_end=2023-01-31
ITEM 1.    BUSINESS
Overview
Salesforce is a global leader in customer relationship management ("CRM") technology that brings companies and their customers together. Founded in 1999, we enable companies of every size and industry to take advantage of powerful technologies to connect to their customers in a whole new way and help them transform their businesses around the customer in this digital-first world.
Our Customer 360 platform unites sales, service, marketing, commerce and IT teams by connecting customer data across systems, apps and devices to create a complete view of customers. With this single source of customer truth, teams can be more responsive, productive and efficient, deliver intelligent, personalized experiences across every channel and increase productivity. With Slack, we provide a digital headquarters where companies, employees, governments and stakeholders can collaborate to create success from anywhere.
Our Customer 360 service offerings are designed to be flexible, scalable and easy to use. They can generally be configured easily, deployed rapidly and integrated with other platforms and enterprise applications. We sell to businesses worldwide, primarily on a subscription basis, through our direct sales efforts and also indirectly through partners. We also enable third parties to use our platform and developer tools to create additional functionality and new applications that run on our platform, which are sold separately from, or in conjunction with, our service offerings.
Salesforce is committed to a core set of values: trust, customer success, innovation, equality and sustainability. Foremost among these is trust, which is the foundation for everything we do. Our customers trust our technology to deliver the highest levels of security, privacy, performance, compliance and availability at scale. Customer success is at the core of our business and we align the entire company around our customers' needs to ensure their success and prove our value. We believe in continuous innovation, enabling our customers to access the latest technology advances so they can innovate and stay ahead in their industries. Equality is a core tenet of how we run our business. We value the equality of every individual at our company and in our communities. We believe that creating a diverse workplace that reflects the communities we serve and fostering an inclusive culture where everyone feels seen, heard and valued makes us a better company. Finally, we believe the world is in a climate crisis and that sustainability, including bold climate action, is the only way forward. We are committed to ambitious climate leadership solutions, and we're bringing the full power of Salesforce to help organizations achieve net zero emissions.
We believe that our values create value, and the business of business is to make the world a better place for all of our stakeholders, including stockholders, customers, employees, partners, the planet and the communities in which we work and live. Salesforce is committed to giving back to our communities, closing the inequality gap and helping businesses grow while protecting the 
…[truncated 18854 chars]…
consumer application companies that have entered the business software market; and
8
Table of Contents
- traditional platform development environment companies and cloud computing development platform companies who may develop toolsets and products that allow customers to build new apps that run on the customers' current infrastructure or as hosted services.
We believe more companies may become competitive threats due to the accelerated shift to cloud and hosted service offerings and customer experience management solutions. We also expect our competition to change and evolve as we expand into more markets, with new offerings.
Customers
We sell to businesses of all sizes and in almost every industry worldwide. The number of paying subscriptions at each of our customers ranges from one to hundreds of thousands. None of our customers accounted for more than five percent of our revenues in fiscal years 2023, 2022 or 2021. In addition, we do not have any material dependencies on any specific product, service or particular group or groups.
Customer Service and Support
We offer professional services to help customers achieve business results faster with Salesforce solutions. Our architects and innovation program teams act as advisors to plan and execute digital transformations for our customers. This includes implementation services for multi-cloud and complex deployments. We provide best-practices and AI-based recommendations and adoption programs globally. In addition, we provide advanced education, including in-person and online courses, to certify our customers and partners on architecting, administering, deploying and developing our service offerings.
Our global customer support group responds to both business and technical inquiries about the use of our products via the web, telephone, email, social networks and other channels. We provide standard customer support during regular business hours at no charge to customers who purchase any of our paying subscription editions. We also offer premier customer support that is either included in a premium success offering or sold for an additional fee, which can include services such as priority access to technical resources, developer support and system administration. In addition, we offer a premier priority support add-on that includes proactive monitoring, rapid incidence response and instruction from a dedicated support team knowledgeable about the customer's specific enterprise architecture.
Sales and Marketing
We sell our services primarily through our direct sales force, which comprises telephone sales personnel based in regional hubs, field sales personnel based in territories close to their customers and self-service offerings.
To a lesser extent, we also utilize a network of partners who refer sales leads to us and assist in selling to these prospects. This network includes global consulting firms, systems integrators and other partners. In return, we typically pay these partners a fee based on the first-year subscription revenue generated by the customers whom they refer. We continue to invest in developing additional distribution channels for our subscription services.
We use a variety of marketing programs across traditional and social channels to target our prospective and current customers, partners and developers. We focus our marketing activities in the cities and countries with the largest market opportunities. Our primary marketing activities include:
- multichannel marketing campaigns that span email, social media, the web, television and more, which align to a broader customer journey;
- in-person and virtual customer events of all sizes to create customer and prospect awareness, including proprietary events such as Dreamforce and our virtual Dreamforce to You, World Tours and other virtual events, as well as participation in trade shows and industry events;
- live events and original programming on our Salesforce+ streaming service, which includes discussions about the future of technology in the digital-first, work anywhere world and educational content to learn new skills and pursue new career opportunities;
- press and industry analyst relations to garner third-party validation and generate positive coverage for our company, brand, service offerings and value proposition;
- partner co-marketing activities with global and regional implementation partners;
- customer testimonials and our community of Trailblazers: individuals who drive innovation, grow their careers and transform their businesses using the Customer 360 platform;
- in-person and virtual technology event sponsorships; and
- event partnerships with high-profile global brands and organizations.
Intellectual Property
We rely on a combination of trademarks, copyrights, trade secrets and patents and contractual provisions, to protect our proprietary technology and our brands. We also enter into confidentiality and proprietary rights agreements with our employees, consultants and other third parties and control access to software, services, documentation and other proprietary information. We believe the duration of our patents is adequate relative to the expected lives of our service offerings. We also purchase or
9
Table of Contents
license technology that we incorporate into our products or services. At times, we make select intellectual property broadly available at no or low cost to achieve a strategic objective, such as promoting industry standards, advancing interoperability, supporting open source software or attracting and enabling our external development community. While it may be necessary in the future to seek or renew licenses relating to various aspects of our products and business methods, we believe, based upon past experience and industry practice, such licenses generally could be obtained on commercially reasonable terms.
Human Capital Management
Salesforce is committed to a core set of values: trust, customer success, innovation, equality and sustainability. These core values are the foundation of our company culture, which we believe is fundamental to, and a competitive advantage in, our approach to managing our workforce. We believe our company culture fosters open dialogue, collaboration, recognition and a sense of family, all of which allow us to attract and retain the best talent, which is critical for our continued success. For example, our sales, engineering and customer success teams are critical to our ability to grow, innovate and ensure the trust and customer success of our customers.
We believe our efforts in managing our workforce have been effective. Our focus on our workplace environment and a strong company culture has led to recognition across the globe, as evidenced by the following awards: Fortune World's Most Admired Companies (2022 and for the eighth year in a row), Fortune 100 Best Companies to Work For (2022 and for the 13th year in a row), Human Rights Campaign Best Places to Work for LGBTQ Equality (2022) and Glassdoor Employees' Choice Best Place to Work in Canada, France, Germany, the United Kingdom and the United States (2022).
As of January 31, 2023, we had 79,390 employees, of which approximately 52 percent were located in the United States and 48 percent were located internationally and approximately 36 percent identified as women, 63 percent identified as men and less than 0.2 percent identified as non-binary. This number includes those individuals impacted by our restructuring plan announced on January 4, 2023, which includes a reduction of our current workforce by approximately 10 percent. None of our employees in the United States are represented by a labor union. However, employees of certain foreign subsidiaries are represented by works councils.

CRM.quantitative_report:
**Quantitative Report: CRM (as of 2024-01-01)**

**1. Financial Trajectory**
- **Revenue:** TTM revenue (sum of last 4 quarters) is $33.95B. Quarterly revenue shows a consistent upward trend, from $7.33B (2022-01-31) to $8.72B (2023-10-31). Latest QoQ growth is +1.4%; YoY growth is +11.3%.
- **Earnings:** TTM net income is $2.59B. Quarterly EPS is volatile: $1.25 (Q3 2023), $1.28 (Q2 2023), $0.20 (Q1 2023), -$0.10 (Q4 2022), $0.21 (Q3 2022). Latest YoY EPS growth is +495.2% (from $0.21). Latest QoQ EPS change is -2.3%.
- **Margins:** Gross margin is strong at ~75.3% (gross profit $6.57B / revenue $8.72B). TTM operating income is $3.75B; operating margin is ~11.0% (per valuation data). Net margin (TTM) is ~7.6% (per valuation data).
- **Cash Flow:** Free cash flow data is unavailable for all reported periods.
- **Balance Sheet:** Total assets $91.02B, total equity $58.09B, total debt $9.43B. Debt-to-equity is 0.162. Equity has been stable, ranging from $57.41B to $60.10B over the past eight quarters.

**2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience**
- **Earnings Quality:** Quarterly net income has been inconsistent, including negative values in Q1 2023 (-$98M) and Q4 2022 (-$28M). The TTM net income of $2.59B masks this volatility. The YoY improvement in EPS from a low base ($0.21 to $1.25) is significant.
- **Balance-Sheet Resilience:** Low leverage (debt-to-equity of 0.162) and stable equity provide a solid buffer. Total assets slightly decreased from $98.85B (2023-01-31) to $91.02B.

**3. Valuation vs. History**
- **Current Multiples (2023-12-29):** PE (diluted) = 99.59, Price-to-Book = 4.444, Price-to-Sales = 7.603.
- **Historical Context (2023 window):** PE ranged from 94.2 to 1063.7. Current PE of 99.59 is near the low end of this range. Price-to-Book ranged from 2.3 to 4.5; the current 4.444 is near the top. Net margin improved from 0.009 (Jan 2023) to 0.076 (Dec 2023).

**4. Price Level & Returns vs. Fundamentals**
- **Price Level:** $263.14 (2023-12-29), near its 52-week high of $268.36 and up from a low of $129.50.
- **Returns:** Quarterly return (Q4 2023) is +29.8%. Annual return (2023) is +104.8%.
- **Fundamental Context:** The stock's price appreciation has outpaced revenue growth (TTM +11.3% YoY) but coincides with significant profitability improvement (net margin from ~0.9% to 7.6%), driving the PE compression from ~900x to ~100x.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside**
- **Upside:** Continued revenue growth and operating leverage could expand margins further, supporting the elevated valuation. The improving profitability trend (net margin 7.6%, ROE 4.5%) is a key positive.
- **Downside:** The valuation remains high (PE ~100x) relative to the ~11% revenue growth and historical margin volatility. Earnings quality is inconsistent, and the lack of cash flow data limits full assessment. A reversion in margin expansion would pressure the valuation.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
The quantitative profile shows accelerating revenue growth and a sharp improvement in profitability metrics, which have driven a substantial re-rating (PE compression). However, the current valuation (PE 99.59, P/B 4.44) is elevated and priced for continued execution. The balance sheet is resilient, but earnings quality is volatile and cash flow evidence is absent. Risk/reward appears balanced, leaning toward caution given the high multiples priced against a history of inconsistent earnings.
CRM.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Assessment Report: Salesforce (CRM)**
**Decision Date: 2024-01-01**

**1. Business/Franchise Read**
Salesforce is characterized as a global leader in customer relationship management (CRM) technology (10-K, 2023-03-08). Its core franchise is the Customer 360 platform, which unites sales, service, marketing, commerce, and IT teams by connecting customer data across systems to create a single source of truth (10-K, 2023-03-08). The business model is primarily subscription-based, delivered via a multi-tenant cloud architecture (10-K, 2023-03-08). Service offerings are extensive, including Sales, Service, Marketing, Commerce, Analytics (Tableau), Integration (MuleSlack), and a data cloud (Genie) (10-K, 2023-03-08). Growth strategy focuses on upselling/cross-selling to existing customers, geographic expansion, industry verticals, and leveraging its partner ecosystem and developer community (10-K, 2023-03-08).

**2. Recent Developments (Dated from Supplied Text)**
*   **Financial Performance & Guidance (2023-11 to 2023-12):** The company reported strong third-quarter results (fiscal year '24) in November 2023, beating Wall Street targets and raising its annual profit forecast, citing steady cloud demand (Web context, 2023-11-29; 2023-12-01). Revenue was reported as soaring 14% to $8.38 billion, with EPS growth of 100% (Web context, 2023-12-07). Raised FY24 guidance to $34.75-$34.8B (Web context, 2023-12-14).
*   **Product & Strategic Narrative (2023-12):** Analyst commentary highlights robust demand for MuleSoft and Data Cloud services and early adoption of AI capabilities as bullish catalysts (Web context, 2023-12-04). The company was noted for launching a new product (AI-focused) that could significantly boost revenue growth (News, 2023-12-29). The narrative frames the company as starting a "new chapter" focused on profitable growth post-shareholder activism (Web context, 2023-12-04; News, 2023-12-29).
*   **Market Sentiment (2023-12):** The stock is frequently listed among recommended growth stocks for 2024 and noted as one that soared most during 2023 (News, 2023-12-30; 2023-12-29). It received at least two bullish upgrades ahead of Q1 2024 (News, 2023-12-29).

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
*   **Risk - Legal & Reputational Exposure:** An ongoing lawsuit (*G.G. v. Salesforce.com, Inc.*, case referenced 2023-12-22) links the company's platform to alleged criminal sex-trafficking activities conducted by users of a defunct third-party site. This presents reputational and potential financial risk.
    *   *Offset:* The company's core values in the 10-K emphasize trust, safety, and ethical use of technology. The specific legal merits were not detailed in the provided text.
*   **Risk - Corporate Governance & Talent:** A report indicates the company reserves the right to fire employees based on political beliefs, with a related SEC petition (Web context, 2023-12-14). This could affect talent attraction/attrition and brand perception.
    *   *Offset:* The company's 10-K highlights a strong, award-winning culture focused on equality, diversity, and inclusion as a competitive advantage, and extensive human capital initiatives (10-K, 2023-03-08).
*   **Risk - Competitive Market:** The 10-K explicitly states the market is "highly competitive, rapidly evolving and fragmented," with competitors ranging from specialized vendors to broad platform giants (10-K, 2023-03-08).
    *   *Offset:* Competitive strength is derived from an integrated platform (Customer 360), a large ecosystem (AppExchange, Trailblazers), and recent momentum in high-demand areas like AI and Data Cloud (Web context, 2023-12-04).

**4. Concise Qualitative Assessment**
**Positive.** The supplied narrative through late 2023 is decidedly bullish. The franchise is identified as a market leader. Recent developments (as of Nov-Dec 2023) are characterized by raised guidance, strong execution on profitability, and a strategic pivot towards growth drivers like AI and Data Cloud. Sentiment from financial media and analyst commentary is enthusiastic. While identifiable risks exist—most prominently a serious legal case and governance controversies—they are offset in the text by the company's stated values, strong cultural accolades, and the prevailing positive momentum around its financial and product performance.

*Assessment based solely on the point-in-time evidence supplied, which for recent developments was heavily weighted toward late 2023 financial results and forward-looking optimism.*

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Salesforce shows strong profitability improvement and raised guidance, but the stock's 100x PE valuation prices in continued execution with little margin for error, creating a balanced risk/reward profile.

IBM.quantitative_report:
**IBM Quantitative Report (Decision Date: 2024-01-01)**

**1. Financial Trajectory**
*   **Revenue & Earnings:** TTM revenue (sum of last 4Q) is $61.17B. Latest Q3 2023 revenue is $14.75B, down -4.7% QoQ but up +4.6% YoY. TTM net income is $6.92B. Latest Q3 2023 net income is $1.70B, up +7.6% QoQ but YoY comparison is distorted by the prior year's Q3 2022 net loss of -$3.20B.
*   **Margins:** Latest Q3 2023 gross profit margin is 54.3% ($8.02B / $14.75B). Latest operating margin is 13.5% ($1.87B / $14.75B). TTM operating income is $8.24B. Latest net margin is 11.3% per valuation ratios.
*   **Cash Flow:** Cash flow statement data is unavailable for all periods provided.
*   **Balance Sheet:** Total equity increased from $19.00B (2021-12-31) to $23.16B (2023-09-30). Total liabilities decreased from $113.00B to $106.17B over the same period. Total assets decreased from $132.00B to $129.32B.

**2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience**
*   **Earnings Quality:** Earnings exhibit significant volatility. A large operating loss of -$4.50B in Q3 2022 led to a net loss of -$3.20B, followed by positive operating income in subsequent quarters. The YoY EPS growth of -152.0% reflects this distortion. TTM earnings are positive at $6.92B.
*   **Balance-Sheet Resilience:** Leverage is high. Total liabilities of $106.17B represent 4.58x total equity ($23.16B). Equity constitutes only 17.9% of total assets ($23.16B / $129.32B).

**3. Valuation Versus History**
*   The latest PE (diluted) is 21.818, within its 1-year range of 19.3 to 104.5, and near the bottom of that range.
*   The latest price-to-book (P/B) ratio is 6.524, within its 1-year range of 5.1 to 6.6, and at the top of that range.
*   The latest price-to-sales (P/S) is 2.47. The latest earnings yield is 4.6%.
*   Valuation metrics (PE, net margin, ROE) improved significantly in late 2023. PE compressed from ~101 (2023-01-03) to ~21 (2023-12-29) as net margin expanded from 0.027 to 0.113 and ROE from 0.063 to 0.299.

**4. Price Level and Return Context**
*   **Latest Price:** $163.55 (2023-12-29). The 52-week range (approx.) is $120.55 to $166.34.
*   **Returns:** The stock returned +16.6% over the last quarter (vs. 2023-09-29) and +16.8% over the last year (vs. 2022-12-28). The quarterly path shows a -7.7% decline in Q1 2023, followed by gains of +3.6%, +4.9%, and +16.6% in subsequent quarters.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside**
*   **Upside:** Continued improvement in operational margins and earnings. TTM operating income is $8.24B. Reduction in leverage, as liabilities have trended down from $114.16B (2022-03-31) to $106.17B.
*   **Downside:** High financial leverage (debt-to-equity of 4.58x) poses a risk. Earnings volatility is demonstrated by the Q3 2022 loss. Revenue growth is modest (+4.6% YoY latest) and showed QoQ contraction.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
The stock trades at a historically low PE (21.8) on improved TTM margins (11.3% net) and earnings, supported by a recent +16.6% quarterly price rally. However, balance-sheet resilience is weak due to very high leverage (4.58x debt-to-equity). The quantitative case is mixed: valuation has improved meaningfully from historically expensive levels, but the fundamental profile carries elevated risk from the capital structure and demonstrated earnings volatility. Cash flow generation cannot be assessed due to unavailable data.
IBM.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Report: IBM (Decision Date: 2024-01-01)**

**1. Business/Franchise Read**
Based on the supplied narrative, IBM's franchise is characterized as a strategic pivot towards higher-margin businesses, with a core focus on **hybrid cloud and artificial intelligence (AI)**. The company leverages its legacy infrastructure, such as mainframes, as a foundation for growth, with AI solutions (e.g., translating aging COBOL code) cited as a key value driver. The business model emphasizes product innovation, regular customer upgrades, and cost-cutting, productivity-boosting solutions.

**2. Recent Developments (Dated)**
*   **Corporate Action / M&A (2023-12-29):** IBM signed a definitive deal to acquire StreamSets and webMethods from Software AG for €2.13 billion (~$2.3 billion) in cash, aimed at bolstering its data integration and AI application capabilities.
*   **Market Narrative / Sentiment (Late Dec 2023):** Multiple financial news articles (2023-12-22, 2023-12-23, 2023-12-24, 2023-12-29, 2023-12-31) identified IBM as a top AI stock, a "screaming bargain," and a recommended buy for 2024, citing its AI capabilities, valuation (17x forward multiple), and 4% dividend yield.
*   **Growth Catalysts (2023-12-22, 2023-12-20):** Web context highlighted continued product innovation, the integration of AI, and growth in platform-as-a-service as catalysts for 2024. A 98% year-over-year surge in mainframe sales (referenced from Q3 2022) was noted as a recent growth driver.

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
*   **Risk: Intellectual Property & Legal Disputes.** IBM faces active litigation, including a copyright lawsuit with Micro Focus (2023-12-15) and a trade secrets lawsuit with GlobalFoundries (2023-12-26), which it failed to halt. These pose reputational and financial risk.
*   **Risk: Employment & Regulatory Litigation.** The company agreed to a $4.75 million settlement over unpaid wages (2023-12-05) and faces legal challenges to its diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI) policies (2023-12-12), indicating operational and compliance risks.
*   **Offset: Strategic Execution.** The recent €2.13 billion acquisition (2023-12-29) demonstrates active capital allocation to strengthen its core hybrid cloud and AI portfolio, potentially accelerating growth.
*   **Offset: Positive Narrative Momentum.** Consistent positive analyst and media sentiment in late 2023, focusing on AI and valuation, may support investor confidence.

**4. Qualitative Assessment**
**Cautiously Positive.** The narrative is dominated by IBM's strategic focus on high-growth areas (AI, hybrid cloud) and supported by a significant acquisition to enhance its capabilities. Positive market sentiment and a focus on shareholder returns (dividend) are clear. However, this is materially offset by a cluster of recent, unresolved legal and regulatory challenges that represent tangible operational and reputational risks. The assessment is positive on strategic direction but tempered by the disclosed legal overhang.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: IBM's valuation has compressed to a historically low PE of 21.8x on improved margins and earnings, and the company is making credible strategic moves in AI and hybrid cloud, including a €2.13B acquisition. However, extremely high leverage (4.58x debt-to-equity), demonstrated earnings volatility, modest revenue growth, and unresolved legal overhangs limit conviction in a sustained re-rating.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: IBM's valuation has compressed to a historically low PE of 21.8x on improved margins and earnings, and the company is making credible strategic moves in AI and hybrid cloud, including a €2.13B acquisition. However, extremely high leverage (4.58x debt-to-equity), demonstrated earnings volatility, modest revenue growth, and unresolved legal overhangs limit conviction in a sustained re-rating.
key_factors: Valuation improvement: PE compressed from ~101x (Jan 2023) to ~21.8x (Dec 2023) as net margin expanded from 2.7% to 11.3% and ROE from 6.3% to 29.9%, placing the stock near the bottom of its 1-year PE range.
High financial leverage: Total liabilities of $106.17B represent 4.58x total equity ($23.16B), with equity only 17.9% of total assets, creating meaningful balance-sheet risk.

Strategic AI/hybrid cloud pivot: The €2.13B acquisition of StreamSets and webMethods (2023-12-29) and consistent positive market narrative around AI capabilities support the growth thesis, though revenue growth remains modest at +4.6% YoY.

Earnings volatility and legal overhang: A -$3.20B net loss in Q3 2022 demonstrates earnings instability, while active litigation including copyright (Micro Focus, 2023-12-15) and trade secrets (GlobalFoundries, 2023-12-26) lawsuits pose reputational and financial risk.

  filing_date=2023-09-07 section=business period_end=2023-07-29
Item 1. Business
General
Cisco designs and sells a broad range of technologies that power the Internet. We are integrating our product portfolios across networking, security, collaboration, applications and the cloud to create highly secure, intelligent platforms for our customers' digital businesses. These platforms are designed to help our customers manage more users, devices and things connecting to their networks. This will enable us to provide customers with a highly secure, intelligent platform for their digital business.
We conduct our business globally and manage our business by geography. Our business is organized into the following three geographic segments: Americas; Europe, Middle East, and Africa (EMEA); and Asia Pacific, Japan, and China (APJC).
Our products and technologies are grouped into the following categories: Secure, Agile Networks; Internet for the Future; Collaboration; End-to-End Security; Optimized Application Experiences; and Other Products. In addition to our product offerings, we provide a broad range of service offerings, including technical support services and advanced services. Increasingly, we are delivering our technologies through software and services. Our custom
…[truncated 20799 chars]…
ross our security portfolio focused on cloud-based offerings, AI-driven threat detection and end-to-end security architectures. Additionally, we continue to invest in expanding our SASE architecture by delivering combined network and security functionality in a single cloud-native service.
5
Table of Contents
Optimized Application Experiences
The Optimized Application Experiences product category consists of our full stack observability and network assurance offerings. Our full stack observability offerings are designed to bring together and provide end-to-end visibility of our customer's environments across applications, networks, multi-cloud infrastructures and the Internet, to help deliver full stack observability for modern environments and drive relevant real-time insights. Our Intersight Infrastructure Services, a cloud-delivered SaaS offering, provides lifecycle operations management, automation, orchestration, and monitoring capabilities for customers' infrastructure deployments from Data Center to the Edge. Our monitoring and analytics offering, AppDynamics, monitors performance across different application-related domains. Our network assurance offering, ThousandEyes, is a network intelligence platform that provides in-depth visibility into network and Internet performance. It enables organizations to see, understand, and improve every digital experience and assure seamless connectivity for their modern digital environments.
Services
In addition to our product offerings, we provide a broad range of service and support options for our customers. Our overall service and support offerings are combined into one organization, Customer Experience, that is responsible for the end-to-end customer experience.
Our support and maintenance services help our customers ensure their products operate efficiently, remain available, and benefit from the most up-to-date system and application software. These services help customers protect their network investments, manage risk, and minimize downtime for systems running mission-critical applications.
We also provide comprehensive advisory services that are focused on responsive, preventive, and consultative support of our technologies for specific networking needs. We are investing in and expanding advisory services in the areas of software, cloud, security, and analytics, which reflects our strategy of selling customer outcomes. We are focused on three priorities: utilizing technology advisory services to drive higher product and services; assessment and migration services providing the tools, expertise and methodologies to enable our customers to migrate to new technology platforms; and providing optimization services aligned with customers' business expectations.
Customers and Markets
Many factors influence the IT, collaboration, and networking requirements of our customers. These include the size of the organization, number and types of technology systems, geographic location, and business applications deployed throughout the customer's network. Our customer base is not limited to any specific industry, geography, or market segment. Our customers primarily operate in the following markets: enterprise, commercial, service provider, and public sector.
Enterprise
Enterprise businesses are large regional, national, or global organizations with multiple locations or branch offices. Many enterprise businesses have unique IT, collaboration, and networking needs within a multivendor environment. We offer service and support packages, financing, and managed network services, primarily through our service provider partners. We sell these products through a network of third-party application and technology vendors and channel partners, as well as selling directly to these customers.
Commercial
The commercial market represents larger, or midmarket and small businesses. We sell to our midmarket customers through a combination of our direct sales force and channel partners. These customers typically require the latest advanced technologies that our enterprise customers demand, but with less complexity. Small businesses require information technologies and communications products that are easy to configure, install, and maintain. We sell to these smaller organizations within the commercial market primarily through channel partners.
Service Providers
Service providers offer data, voice, video, and mobile/wireless services to businesses, governments, utilities, and consumers worldwide. The service provider market includes regional, national, and international wireline carriers, webscale operators as well as Internet, cable, and wireless providers. We also include media, broadcast, and content providers within our service provider market, as the lines in the telecommunications industry continue to blur between traditional network-based, content-based and application-based services. Service providers use a variety of our products and services for their own networks. In addition, many service providers use Cisco data center, virtualization, and collaboration technologies to offer managed or Internet-based services to their business customers. Compared with other customers, service providers are more likely to require network design, deployment, and support services because of the greater scale and higher complexity of their networks, whose requirements are addressed, we believe, by our architectural approach.
6
Table of Contents
Public Sector
The public sector market includes federal, state and local governments, as well as educational institution customers. Many public sector customers have unique IT, collaboration, and networking needs within a multi-vendor environment. We sell to public sector customers through a network of third-party application and technology vendors, channel partners, as well as through direct sales.
Sales Overview
As of the end of fiscal 2023, our worldwide sales and marketing functions consisted of approximately 26,000 employees, including managers, sales representatives, and technical support personnel. We sell our products and services both directly and indirectly through a variety of channels with support from our salesforce. A substantial portion of our products and services is sold indirectly through channel partners, and the remainder is sold through direct sales. Channel partners include systems integrators, service providers, other third-party resellers, and distributors.
Systems integrators and service providers typically sell directly to end users and often provide system installation, technical support, professional services, and other support services in addition to network equipment sales. Systems integrators also typically integrate our products into an overall solution. Some service providers are also systems integrators.
Distributors may hold inventory and sell to systems integrators, service providers, and other third-party resellers. We refer to sales through distributors as our two-tier system of sales to the end customer. Revenue from two-tier distributors is recognized based on a sell-in method. These distributors may be given business terms that allow them to return a limited portion of inventory, receive credits for changes in selling prices, receive certain rebates, and participate in various cooperative marketing programs.
For information regarding risks related to our sales channels, see "Item 1A. CSCO.quantitative_report:
### Quantitative Analysis: CSCO (as of 2024-01-01)

**1. Financial Trajectory**
- **Revenue:** TTM revenue of $58.03B. Latest quarter (Q1 2024) revenue $14.67B, down -3.5% QoQ from $15.20B but up +7.6% YoY from $13.63B.
- **Earnings & Margins:** TTM net income $13.58B. Latest diluted EPS $0.89, down -7.3% QoQ but up +36.9% YoY. TTM gross margin (gross profit/revenue) is 63.7%. TTM operating margin (operating income/revenue) is 27.2%. TTM net margin is 23.4%.
- **Balance Sheet:** Total assets of $98.78B, down slightly from $101.85B prior quarter. Total debt decreased to $7.70B from $8.40B QoQ. Total equity increased to $45.21B from $44.35B QoQ. Debt-to-equity ratio is 0.17.
- **Cash Flow:** Historical free cash flow and operating cash flow data are unavailable.

**2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience**
- **Earnings Quality:** Net margin expanded to 23.4% in the latest quarter from 20.9% a year prior, indicating improved profitability. ROE is 30.0%. However, the absence of cash flow data prevents analysis of cash conversion.
- **Balance-Sheet Resilience:** The balance sheet is strong with low leverage (debt-to-equity 0.17). Debt has been reduced over the past two years from $9.50B to $7.70B. Equity has grown steadily from $39.50B to $45.21B over the same period.

**3. Valuation vs. History**
- **Current Multiples:** P/E (diluted) of 15.20, Price-to-Book of 4.57, Price-to-Sales of 3.56.
- **Historical Context:** The P/E has compressed from 17.16 at the start of 2023 to its current level, and is near the lower end of its 12-month range (14.4 to 20.7). The P/B has declined from 4.90 to 4.57 over the year, also near the lower end of its range (4.3 to 5.6). The earnings yield of 6.6% is at the high end of its recent history.

**4. Price Level & Return Context**
- **Price:** Closed at $50.52 on 2023-12-29, within a 52-week range of $45.55 to $58.19.
- **Returns:** The stock returned -6.0% over the last quarter and +7.3% over the last year. The annual return trails the strong YoY earnings growth of +36.9%.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside**
- **Upside:** The stock's valuation is near the low end of its historical range (P/E 15.2 vs. range high 20.7). Strong YoY earnings growth (+36.9%) and a robust, deleveraging balance sheet provide fundamental support.
- **Downside:** A sequential revenue and earnings decline (-3.5% and -7.3% QoQ) suggests potential near-term momentum headwinds. The stock has already underperformed its earnings growth, and further multiple compression is possible if growth decelerates.

**6. Provisional Risk/Reward Conclusion**
The quantitative profile presents a mixed picture. CSCO demonstrates strong fundamental improvement over the past year, with accelerating earnings growth and a resilient balance sheet. Valuation multiples have compressed and sit at the lower end of their recent ranges, potentially reflecting priced-in risk for the observed sequential slowdown. The annual return has lagged strong earnings growth. The risk is that the sequential deceleration continues; the reward is that the market has already discounted this and the valuation re-rates toward historical norms if growth stabilizes. The balance sheet strength limits downside risk.
CSCO.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Assessment Report: Cisco Systems (CSCO)**
**Decision Date: 2024-01-01 | Evidence Cutoff: <2024-01-01**

**1. Business/Franchise Read (Source: 10-K, filing_date=2023-09-07)**
Cisco is a global technology leader designing and selling a broad range of networking, security, collaboration, and cloud technologies to power the Internet. The company operates across three geographic segments (Americas, EMEA, APJC) and serves diverse customers including enterprises, service providers, and the public sector. Its core strategy is to "securely connect everything" by helping customers transform infrastructure, secure enterprises, enable hybrid work, and reimagine applications. A key ongoing transformation involves shifting its business model towards software and subscription-based offerings (e.g., Cisco DNA, Cisco Plus) to provide more flexible consumption models. The franchise is characterized by integrated platforms, a wide product portfolio (Secure Agile Networks, Internet for the Future, Collaboration, End-to-End Security, Optimized Application Experiences), and a broad services organization.

**2. Recent Narrative Developments (Dated from Supplied Text)**
*   **Positive Financial & Strategic Momentum:**
    *   **2023-11-15:** Cisco reported Q1 2024 results with "record revenue and profitability," delivering performance at the high end or above its guidance range. Strength was noted in software revenue, product ARR, and total RPO, with management citing foundational business strength and growth opportunities fueled by AI, Security, Cloud, and Observability.
    *   **2023-12-04:** Cisco's acquisition of Splunk is highlighted by analysts as a strategic move expected to significantly expand its software revenue, aligning with favorable industry trends.
*   **Competitive & Market Concerns:**
    *   **2023-12-22:** Cisco was named "Bear of the Day" by Zacks, citing significant challenges including slowing growth, a cutthroat competitive landscape, and a weak industry group.
    *   **2023-12-28:** News regarding competitor Extreme Networks notes an ongoing "inventory correction" masking growth in the networking sector.
*   **Corporate & Legal Matters:**
    *   **2023-12-11:** Cisco settled a False Claims Act suit for $8.6 million related to product vulnerabilities, a noted warning for security companies.
    *   **2023-12-13:** In a positive legal outcome, a U.S. District Judge ruled that Cisco did not infringe on Centripetal's patents, rejecting a prior large damages award.
    *   **2023-12-19:** Reports noted insider selling activity at Cisco, though the context (pre-planned vs. sentiment-driven) was not specified.

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
*   **Risks:**
    *   **Competitive & Growth Pressure:** Public analyst commentary (2023-12-22) explicitly warns of slowing growth and intense competition, which could pressure market share and margins. Broader networking sector inventory corrections (2023-12-28) may also impact near-term demand.
    *   **Regulatory & Legal Liability:** The $8.6M false claims settlement (2023-12-11) establishes a precedent for liability related to product vulnerabilities, potentially increasing compliance costs or reputational risk in the security segment.
    *   **Corporate Governance Perception:** Notable insider sales (2023-12-19) can be a negative signal to the market regarding management's view of the company's prospects or valuation.
*   **Offsets:**
    *   **Execution on Software Transition & Innovation:** Strong Q1 2024 results (2023-11-15) demonstrate current operational strength and progress in the strategic shift to software and subscriptions. Continued investment in key growth vectors (AI, Security, Cloud) is evident.
    *   **Strategic Portfolio Enhancement:** The pending Splunk acquisition (2023-12-04) is viewed as a catalyst for expanding high-margin software revenue and data observability capabilities.
    *   **Resolution of Key Legal Risk:** The favorable ruling in the Centripetal patent case (2023-12-13) removes a significant, multi-hundred-million-dollar overhang.

**4. Concise Qualitative Assessment**
**Neutral with Positive Momentum.** The recent narrative presents a balanced picture. Cisco's fundamental business shows positive momentum, evidenced by strong quarterly execution (2023-11-15) and a strategically significant acquisition (2023-12-04) that advances its software transformation. However, this is tempered by clear external risks, including public analyst concerns about a fiercely competitive landscape and slowing growth (2023-12-22), alongside the inherent challenges of integrating a major acquisition. The resolution of a major patent lawsuit (2023-12-13) is a material positive offset to recent legal costs (2023-12-11). The qualitative evidence suggests a company executing on its transition but operating in a challenging market environment.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Cisco presents a balanced risk/reward profile: strong YoY earnings growth (+36.9%) and a compressed valuation (P/E 15.2 near 12-month low) are offset by sequential deceleration (-3.5% QoQ revenue) and competitive headwinds. The pending Splunk acquisition and software transition provide upside optionality, but near-term momentum uncertainty warrants a cautious Hold.

The pending Splunk acquisition and software transition provide upside optionality, but near-term momentum uncertainty warrants a cautious Hold.
key_factors: Valuation near historical low end: P/E of 15.20 vs. 12-month range of 14.4-20.7, with earnings yield of 6.6% at the high end of recent history, suggesting limited downside priced in.
Strong YoY fundamental improvement: TTM net margin expanded to 23.4% from 20.9% a year prior, diluted EPS grew +36.9% YoY, and debt reduced from $9.50B to $7.70B over two years.

Sequential momentum deceleration: Q1 2024 revenue declined -3.5% QoQ and EPS fell -7.3% QoQ, with analyst concerns about slowing growth and inventory corrections in the networking sector (2023-12-22, 2023-12-28).
Strategic catalyst from Splunk acquisition (2023-12-04) expected to expand software revenue, complementing strong Q1 2024 execution with record revenue and profitability (2023-11-15).

HON.quantitative_report:
**HON Quantitative Assessment (PIT: 2024-01-01)**

**1. Financial Trajectory**
*   **Revenue:** Steady quarterly growth, rising from $8.95B (Q2 2022) to $9.21B (Q3 2023). TTM revenue = $36.41B.
*   **Earnings:** TTM net income = $5.45B. Quarterly EPS trajectory shows improvement: $1.84 (Q2 2022) to $2.27 (Q3 2023). YoY growth was modest: Q3 2023 EPS of $2.27 vs. $2.28 prior year.
*   **Margins:** TTM gross margin = 37.1% (13.49B / 36.41B). TTM operating margin = 18.9% (6.88B / 36.41B). TTM net margin = 15.0%. Gross profit showed significant YoY growth in Q3 2023 (+19.3%).
*   **Cash Flow:** Not available.
*   **Balance Sheet:** Moderate leverage. Total debt increased from $16.06B (Q4 2021) to $18.35B (Q3 2023). Total equity slightly declined from $19.24B to $17.77B over the same period. Debt-to-equity = 1.03.

**2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience**
*   **Earnings Quality:** Supported by consistent revenue and gross margin expansion. The YoY EPS dip (-0.4%) despite revenue growth (+2.9%) suggests rising costs or one-time items in the prior year (Q3 2022 EPS: $2.28). The reliability of net income is consistent (ranging $1.02B-$1.55B over 8 quarters).
*   **Balance-Sheet Resilience:** Leverage is moderate (debt-to-equity 1.03). However, the trend shows increasing debt alongside slightly declining equity, reducing financial flexibility. Total assets ($61.30B) comfortably exceed total liabilities ($43.52B).

**3. Valuation vs. History**
*   **P/E (Diluted):** At 24.2x, trading above the 1-year historical LOW of 20.3x but below the HIGH of 26.4x and the range midpoint (~23.4x). The historical series shows a range of 20.5x (Oct 2023) to 25.5x (Jan 2023).
*   **P/B:** At 7.4x, near the top of its 1-year range (6.2x - 7.6x) and above most historical points (e.g., 6.287 in Oct 2023).
*   **Earnings Yield:** 4.1% (1/24.2x P/E).
*   **Assessment:** Valuation multiples are at the higher end of their recent historical ranges. The +19.3% YoY gross profit growth supports a premium, but the flat YoY EPS growth tempers this.

**4. Price & Returns Context**
*   **Latest Price:** $197.65 (Dec 29, 2023).
*   **Quarterly Return:** +13.5% (from $174.12 in late Sep 2023).
*   **Annual Return:** -1.2% (from $199.99 in late Dec 2022).
*   **52-Week Range:** $164.83 - $204.73. The current price is near the top of this range.
*   **Context:** The strong Q4 2023 rally (+13.5%) meets fundamental improvement (sequential revenue/earnings growth and margin expansion) but occurs after a year of flat price performance (-1.2%) despite positive TTM growth.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside**
*   **Upside:** Consistent revenue growth and expanding margins (TTM gross margin 37.1%, operating margin 18.9%). Strong quarterly momentum in Q3 2023 (EPS +2.3% QoQ).
*   **Downside:** Stagnant YoY earnings growth (Q3 2023 EPS -0.4% YoY). Rising debt ($18.35B) relative to equity ($17.77B). Elevated P/B ratio at 7.4x. Annual price return is negative (-1.2%).

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
Fundamentals show steady revenue growth and high, stable margins, supported by a solid (though levering) balance sheet. Recent quarterly performance and margin expansion are positive. However, the stock's near 52-week high price and elevated P/B valuation incorporate this good performance, leaving limited margin of safety. The primary quantitative risk is the disconnect between robust margins and generic earnings growth, combined with high leverage. Reward is contingent on the sustainability of margin trajectories against a potentially expensive (P/B 7.4x) valuation.
HON.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Report: Honeywell International Inc. (HON)**
**Decision Date: 2024-01-01**

**1. Business/Franchise Read**
From the supplied narrative, Honeywell is characterized as "one of the strongest multi-industry firms in operation today." Its strategic franchise is built on capturing multiple ESG and industrial megatrends, including automation, the future of aviation, and energy transition (2023-12-31). A specific growth engine is the Honeywell Connected Enterprise (HCE) segment, which has grown at a 15% CAGR since 2019 to approximately $1.5 billion in revenue, with robust 2023 orders growth of ~20% (2023-12-11, 2023-12-10).

**2. Recent Developments (Point-in-Time)**
- **Corporate Action / Acquisition (2023-12-11):** Honeywell agreed to acquire Carrier's Global Access Solutions business for $4.95 billion. This is positioned to make Honeywell "a leading provider of security solutions for the digital age," augmenting its growth profile for 2024-25 (2023-12-19).
- **Outlook Guidance (2023-12-31):** Management expressed confidence that the portfolio is "well positioned to accelerate both our top line and earnings growth" in 2024, driven by the noted megatrends.
- **Capital Allocation & Profile:** The company has a 13-year track record of annual dividend increases, with a yield noted at 2.27% (2023-11-21).

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
- **Risk - Legal/Compliance & Regulatory (2023-12-14, 2023-11-16):** The company has faced recent resolutions, including a >$160m matter related to the Foreign Corrupt Practices Act (FCPA) in Brazil, highlighting risks of third-party corruption. Separately, a lawsuit involves alleged contractual duress related to N95 mask sales. These events indicate ongoing legal and compliance exposure.
- **Risk - Competitive/Industry Dynamics (2023-12-28):** News of a major acquisition in the aerospace software space (Synopsys/Ansys) suggests an active competitive landscape in key Honeywell markets.
- **Offset - Strategic Positioning (Multiple Dates):** Evidence points to a deliberate portfolio pivot towards high-growth, technology-linked industrial themes (automation, digital security, connected aircraft), which is presented as an offset to legacy industrial cyclicality.

**4. Concise Qualitative Assessment**
**Positive.** The confirmed evidence points to a franchise with clear, actionable catalysts: a transformative, accretive acquisition in security, strong organic growth momentum in the high-margin HCE segment, and strategic alignment with durable industrial megatrends. While notable compliance risks and legal issues are present, the operational narrative for 2024-25 growth, supported by management's confident outlook and recent capital deployment, dominates the qualitative picture as of the decision date.

*   **Path Context:** Price movement reflects volatility, with a -11.7% decline in the quarter preceding the latest period, followed by a rebound. The +11.8% annual return occurred alongside TTM revenue growth to $25.01B and a P/E contraction from ~33 to ~26.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside**
*   **Upside Quantitative Drivers:** 1) Sustained double-digit YoY revenue and EPS growth (Rev: +14.0%, EPS: +18.3%). 2) Expanding profitability margins (Net Margin rising to 0.333). 3) Current P/E (26.3) is in the lower half of its annual range, potentially reflecting reduced multiple risk.
*   **Downside Quantitative Risks:** 1) Negative shareholder equity (-$4.85B) indicating significant financial leverage. 2) Unknown cash flow generation capability and debt trajectory, which is critical given the leverage. 3) Valuation support is dependent on continued high-margin earnings growth in a leveraged business.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
The fundamental earnings trajectory is strong, with accelerating revenue, expanding margins, and a lower P/E relative to its 2023 range. However, this performance is set against a balance sheet with negative equity, and the absence of cash flow data precludes analysis of debt servicing and shareholder returns capacity. The reward is tied to the durable continuation of earnings growth at a valuation not at historical highs. The risk is concentrated in the unknowns of cash generation and balance sheet sustainability. The quantitative picture is one of strong profitability against a background of significant leverage and information gaps.
MCD.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Assessment Report: McDonald’s (MCD)**
**As of: 2024-01-01**

**1. Business/Franchise Read**
McDonald's is characterized as a mature, stable restaurant franchise with a robust digital ecosystem and a unique financial model. A key point from the supplied text is its revenue generation efficiency; a 2023-12-31 news summary states McDonald's generates over 70 times more revenue from one source (implied to be franchising) than a larger competitor. The company is actively investing in strengthening its physical presence and digital ecosystem to drive growth (2023-12-18).

**2. Recent Developments (Dated)**
*   **Growth Strategy & Capital Allocation (2023-12-06 to 2023-12-14):** McDonald's announced its most ambitious expansion plan, targeting over 50,000 restaurants by 2027 (up from ~40,000) with the opening of nearly 10,000 new locations. This was detailed in multiple reports (e.g., 2023-12-07 Reuters). The plan is expected to drive nearly 2% system-wide sales growth from new restaurants in 2024 and push operating profitability toward 50% of sales by 2027.
*   **Technology & Partnerships (2023-12-20):** The company expanded its partnership with Accenture to strengthen its digital ecosystem, focusing on increased efficiency and quicker problem-solving to support its tech strategy.
*   **Product & Marketing (2023-12-23):** McDonald's announced a new Happy Meal promotion featuring Squishmallows toys, indicating ongoing efforts to leverage popular culture for family traffic.

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
*   **Risk: Legal & Regulatory Challenges.** Multiple lawsuits and regulatory actions are noted in the evidence period.
    *   A class-action lawsuit alleges misleading advertising concerning surcharges on orange juice in combos (2023-12-27).
    *   The company faces ongoing joint-employer liability risks. A new NLRB rule is reported to threaten the franchise business model (2023-11-15), and McDonald's is appealing a case to the Supreme Court on no-poach clauses (2023-11-29). Another suit alleges joint-employer harassment liability (2023-12-21).
    *   A lawsuit targets advertising to children in Quebec (2023-12-17).
*   **Risk: Macroeconomic Environment.** A challenging macroeconomic environment is cited as a concern for the stock (2023-12-18).
*   **Offset: Strong Financial Position & Analyst Sentiment.** The franchise model is characterized as highly profitable. Wall Street analysts maintain an optimistic perspective based on robust financial performance and sales growth trends (2023-12-08).

**4. Qualitative Assessment**
**Positive.** The qualitative narrative is dominated by a clear, ambitious, and capital-allocated growth strategy centered on unit expansion and digital/AI enhancement, which is intended to drive significant margin and revenue growth. The core franchise model is described as strongly profitable. This positive, forward-looking operational narrative is partially counterbalanced by notable qualitative risks related to pending labor regulations and multiple active lawsuits that challenge its franchise and employment models. The assessment is positive as the development news is specific, strategic, and recent, while the identified risks, though material, are presented as ongoing litigation and regulatory challenges rather than new, company-specific operational failures.

Latest 10-K business section (newest first):
  filing_date=2023-03-15 section=business period_end=2023-01-29
Item 1.  Business.
INTRODUCTION
The Home Depot, Inc. is the world's largest home improvement retailer based on net sales for fiscal 2022. We offer our customers a wide assortment of building materials, home improvement products, lawn and garden products, décor products, and facilities maintenance, repair and operations products. We also provide a number of services, including home improvement installation services and tool and equipment rental. As of the end of fiscal 2022, we operated 2,322 stores located throughout the U.S. (including the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico and the territories of the U.S. Virgin Islands and Guam), Canada, and Mexico. The Home Depot stores average approximately 104,000 square feet of enclosed space, with approximately 24,000 additional square feet of outside garden area. We also maintain a network of distribution and fulfillment centers, as well as a number of e-commerce websites in the U.S., Canada and Mexico. When we refer to "The Home Depot," the "Company," "we," "us" or "our" in this report, we are referring to The Home Depot, Inc. and its consolidated subsidiaries.
The Home Depot, Inc. is a Delaware corporation that was incorporated in 1978. Our Store Support Center (corporate headquarters) is located at 2455 Paces Ferry Road, Atlanta, Georgia 30339. Our telephone number at that address is (770) 433-8211.
OUR BUSINESS
OUR STRATEGY
The retail landscape has changed rapidly over the past several years, with customer expectations constantly evolving. In fiscal 2022, we continued to operate with agility to meet the challenges created by a fluid domestic and global business environment, including supply chain disruptions, tight labor market conditions, and ongoing inflationary pressures. Our ability to operate successfully and meet the needs of our customers was due in significant part to our investments over the past several years aimed at creating an interconnected, frictionless shopping experience that enables our customers to seamlessly blend the digital and physical worlds. Going forward, we will leverage the momentum of these investments and continue to invest in our business in support of the fol
…[truncated 19851 chars]…
HAIN
We continue to focus on building best-in-class competitive advantages in our supply chain to be responsive to our customers' expectations for how, when and where they choose to receive our products and services. As part of enhancing the interconnected shopping experience, we continue to invest in expanding our supply chain network, with the goal of achieving the fastest, most efficient and most reliable delivery capabilities in home improvement. Our efforts are focused on ensuring product availability and increasing the speed and reliability of delivery for our customers while managing our costs. Our supply chain investments have helped us to operate effectively and meet our customers' needs throughout the challenging environment over the past few years.
We centrally forecast and replenish the vast majority of our store products through sophisticated inventory management systems and utilize our network of distribution centers to serve both our stores' and customers' needs. Our supply chain includes multiple distribution center platforms in the U.S., Canada, and Mexico tailored to meet the needs of our stores and customers based on types of products, location, transportation, and delivery requirements. These platforms include rapid deployment centers, stocking distribution centers, bulk distribution centers, and direct fulfillment centers, among others. As part of the expansion of our supply chain, we have invested to further automate and mechanize our rapid deployment center network to drive efficiency and faster movement of product.
We are also continuing to expand our fulfillment network, investing in a significant number of new fulfillment facilities to drive speed and reliability of delivery for our customers and to help us ultimately meet our goal of reaching 90% of the U.S. population with same or next day delivery for extended home improvement product offerings, including big and bulky products. These facilities include omni-channel fulfillment centers, which deliver product directly to customers, and market delivery operations, which function as local hubs to consolidate freight for dispatch to customers for the final mile of delivery, with a focus on appliances. In fiscal 2022, we realized our goal to control more of our appliance delivery end-to-end and began managing all of our appliance delivery volume through our market delivery operations. We have also added flatbed distribution centers, which handle large items like lumber and building materials that are transported on flatbed trucks. As of the end of fiscal 2022, we have opened a number of additional fulfillment facilities, and we will continue to build out our fulfillment network to support our business. Our network is designed to create a competitive advantage with unique, industry-leading capabilities for home improvement needs for both Pros and consumers.
In addition to our distribution and fulfillment centers, we leverage our stores as a network of convenient customer pickup, return, and delivery fulfillment locations. Our premium real estate footprint provides a distinct structural and competitive advantage. For customers who shop online and wish to pick up or return merchandise at, or have merchandise delivered from, our stores, we have implemented four interconnected retail programs: BOSS, BOPIS, BODFS, and BORIS. We also provide curbside pickup to complement our BOPIS offerings, in addition to the self-service lockers at the front entrance of many of our stores. We also offer express car and van delivery service that covers over 80% of the U.S. population. For fiscal 2022, approximately 50% of our U.S. online orders were fulfilled through a store. We also continue to focus on developing new capabilities to improve both efficiency and customer experience in our store delivery program. Our strategic intent is to have a portfolio of efficient, timely and reliable sources and methods of delivery to choose from, optimizing order fulfillment and delivery based on customer needs, inventory locations and available transportation options.
Fiscal 2022 Form 10-K 5
Table of Contents
CORPORATE RESPONSIBILITY AND HUMAN CAPITAL MANAGEMENT
We view environmental, social and governance matters through the lens of our business, with an understanding that if we support our associates, our customers, our supplier partners, and the communities we serve, we also support our business and create long-term value for our shareholders. As a result, we believe that ESG is fundamentally embedded in our operations and culture. We organize our efforts around three pillars: (1) Focus on Our People, (2) Operate Sustainably, and (3) Strengthen Our Communities. Highlights of each of these pillars are set forth below. For further information on our three pillars and other ESG-related matters, see our annual ESG Report, available on our website at https://corporate.homedepot.com/responsibility.
Focus on Our People
Our culture and our associates provide intangible and hard-to-replicate competitive advantages, which have been key to helping us navigate challenging market conditions. Our associates are essential to providing the experience and service that our customers demand. To preserve and protect that customer experience, we focus on cultivating a compelling associate experience, which we believe supports our ability to attract and retain our associates. This includes investing in competitive wages and benefits while also providing the culture, tools, training and development opportunities that make working at The Home Depot an enjoyable and rewarding experience. These actions are the foundation of our key tenets of putting customers first and taking care of our associates.
Culture and Values.
The Home Depot has a strong commitment to ethics and integrity, and we are a values- and culture-centric business. Our commitment to our core values drives our approach to human capital management. Our culture is based on our servant leadership philosophy represented by the inverted pyramid, which puts primary importance on our customers and our associates by positioning them at the top, with senior management at the base in a support role. We bring our culture to life through our core values, which serve as the foundation of our business and as the guiding principles behind the decisions we make every day.

Our values also guide our efforts to create an environment that will help us attract and retain skilled associates in the competitive marketplace for talent. We empower our associates to deliver a superior customer experience by living our values, and we position our associates to embody our core values by integrating the importance of our culture into ongoing development programs, performance management practices, and rewards programs. Leaders participate in programs designed to build and strengthen our culture, such as training on leadership skills, cross-functional collaboration, inclusiveness, and associate engagement, and all associates receive annual training on unconscious bias. Our core values are at the root of our human capital management programs.
Our Workforce.
At the end of fiscal 2022, we employed approximately 471,600 associates, of whom approximately 46,500 were salaried, with the remainder compensated on an hourly basis. Set forth below is the geographic makeup of our workforce:

| Canada | 34,500 | 7.3% |
| Mexico | 17,900 | 3.8% |
| Other (1) | 300 | 0.1% |
| Total | 471,600 | 100% |

(1)    Includes associates in our sourcing organization located in China, Vietnam, India, Italy, Poland and Turkey.

HD.quantitative_report:
**Quantitative Assessment: HD (Home Depot) as of 2024-01-01**

**1. Financial Trajectory**
*   **Revenue & Earnings:** Latest Q3 2023 revenue was $37.71B, down 12.1% QoQ (vs. $42.92B) and down 3.0% YoY (vs. $38.87B). Q3 EPS diluted was $3.81, down 18.1% QoQ (vs. $4.65) and down 10.1% YoY (vs. $4.24). TTM revenue was $153.71B with TTM net income of $15.70B.
*   **Margins:** Q3 gross margin was 33.8% ($12.74B / $37.71B). Operating margin was 14.3% ($5.41B / $37.71B). Net margin was 10.1% ($3.81B / $37.71B). The valuation data shows net margin stable at 10.2% and gross margin at 33.4%.
*   **Cash Flow:** No cash flow statement data was supplied.
*   **Balance Sheet:** As of Q3 2023, total assets were $75.58B against total liabilities of $74.15B, yielding total equity of $1.43B.

**2. Earnings Quality & Balance Sheet Resilience**
*   **Earnings Quality:** Unassessable due to the absence of cash flow statement data.
*   **Balance Sheet Resilience:** Low. The equity cushion is minimal. The ratio of total liabilities to total equity was 51.9x ($74.15B / $1.43B). Equity has been volatile, registering as low as -$1.71B in Q1 2022 and $237.0M in Q2 2022.

**3. Valuation vs. Supplied History**
*   As of 2023-12-29, the diluted P/E was 22.046. This is at the top of its provided 365-day range of 16.8 to 22.5.
*   The Price-to-Book ratio was 242.1. This metric is heavily distorted by the volatile and often minimal equity balance, ranging from 184.3 to 934.2 over the period.
*   The earnings yield was 4.5% (1 / 22.046).

**4. Price Level & Return Context vs. Fundamentals**
*   The price closed at $346.55 on 2023-12-29, near its 365-day high of $354.92.
*   Recent price momentum was positive: a quarterly (3-month) return of +14.7% and an annual return of +9.8%.
*   This price strength occurred against a backdrop of negative fundamental momentum, with both QoQ and YoY declines in revenue and earnings.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside**
*   **Upside:** The strongest quantitative signal is the positive recent price momentum (+14.7% quarterly return) occurring as the P/E expanded to the top of its historical range (22.046 vs. range of 16.8-22.5), indicating sustained market appetite.
*   **Downside:** The strongest quantitative risk is the combination of weakening fundamental growth (YoY EPS -10.1%) with a highly leveraged balance sheet (liabilities/equity of 51.9x). Equity provides a minimal buffer against operational or financial setbacks.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
The risk/reward profile appears unfavorable. The stock is valued at the high end of its historical P/E range (22.046) while exhibiting negative trends in revenue and earnings. Furthermore, the balance sheet holds minimal equity, increasing financial risk. The positive price action is disconnected from the deteriorating fundamental trajectory, suggesting valuation expansion rather than fundamental support as the driver of recent returns.
HD.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Assessment: Home Depot (HD)**
**Evidence Point-in-Time:** Strictly through 2023-12-30; Decision Date 2024-01-01

### 1. Business/Franchise Read
Based on the 2023-03-15 10-K filing, Home Depot is the world's largest home improvement retailer. Its business is built on a broad assortment of building materials, home improvement, and décor products, complemented by installation services and tool rentals. It operates 2,322 stores across North America.

**Core Strategic Pillars (2023-03-15 10-K):**
*   **Customer Focus:** Targets two primary groups: DIY consumers and Professional customers (Pros), with specific initiatives (Pro Xtra loyalty, dedicated sales force) to serve and grow the Pro segment, especially for planned purchases.
*   **Interconnected Retail:** Strategy to create a seamless "One Home Depot" experience blending digital and physical channels. Investments are directed at a frictionless customer journey, enhanced by a digital platform, mobile apps, and a reimagined store experience.
*   **Supply Chain as Advantage:** Operates a sophisticated supply chain with multiple distribution models (rapid deployment, fulfillment centers). A key goal is achieving same/next-day delivery to 90% of the U.S. population for extended product offerings, leveraging stores for fulfillment (e.g., ~50% of U.S. online orders fulfilled from stores in FQ22).
*   **Professional Customer Expansion:** The 2020 acquisition of HD Supply extended its reach into the MRO (Maintenance, Repair, and Operations) market for multifamily, hospitality, and healthcare facilities.
*   **Capital Allocation Discipline:** Prioritizes reinvestment in the business for above-market growth, paying and growing its dividend, and then returning excess cash via share repurchases.

### 2. Recent Developments (Dated)
*   **Q3 2023 Earnings Resilience (2023-11-14):** The company reported better-than-expected third-quarter earnings and revenue. An Investing.com article notes an annualized revenue growth rate of 8.5%, primarily driven by comparable-store sales growth rather than new store expansion. Free cash flow was reported to have "surged."
*   **Continued Strategic Execution (2023-12-21):** News indicates that progress on the "One Home Depot" plan and interconnected retail strategy is fostering growth. Sales via digital platforms rose 5% year-over-year in the third quarter of fiscal 2023.
*   **Legal/Regulatory Settlement (2023-12-06):** Home Depot agreed to pay $27 million to settle California state charges related to improper disposal of hazardous waste and privacy violations.
*   **Historical Litigation (Ongoing as of 2023-12-15):** The company is appealing a decision in its effort to recover $50 million in insurance coverage related to the 2014 data breach, indicating a long-standing financial and legal risk from that incident.

### 3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets
**Risks:**
*   **Legal & Regulatory Exposure:** Active litigation exists, including a significant pre-existing data breach insurance case (2023-12-15). The recent $27M settlement in California (2023-12-06) and other pending lawsuits contribute to ongoing legal cost and reputational risk.
*   **Macroeconomic Sensitivity:** The business is subject to the health of the housing market and consumer discretionary spending, which are influenced by factors like interest rates and inflation.
*   **Intense Competition:** The 10-K (2023-03-15) describes a "highly competitive, very fragmented" industry with competition from a wide range of channels and business models, facilitated by internet price transparency.
*   **Execution of Interconnected Strategy:** Success requires continuous, significant investment in technology and supply chain capabilities to meet rising customer expectations for speed and integration.

**Offsets:**
*   **Strategic Momentum:** Recent news (2023-12-21) and earnings (2023-11-14) suggest the interconnected retail and Pro customer strategies are yielding results, with digital sales growing and overall results outperforming forecasts.
*   **Scale and Structural Advantages:** The franchise benefits from the largest store network in the category, a highly recognized brand, and a supply chain network that is a cited competitive moat (10-K, 2023-03-15).
*   **Balanced Capital Allocation:** The adherence to a disciplined framework for reinvestment and shareholder returns (dividends, buybacks) provides a clear discipline for capital deployment (10-K, 2023-03-15).

### 4. Concise Qualitative Assessment
**Neutral.** The franchise remains a category-dominant leader with a clear and apparently well-executed strategy for interconnected retail and Pro customer growth, as evidenced by recent positive earnings reactions and digital sales momentum. However, this is counterbalanced by material and ongoing legal risks—including fresh regulatory settlements and pre-existing breach-related litigation—that pose potential financial and reputational costs. The company's performance is also tied to cyclical macroeconomic factors in a intensely competitive landscape. The qualitative picture is one of strong strategic positioning contrasted with concrete, identifiable legal headwinds.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Home Depot is a category-dominant franchise with strong strategic execution, but the stock is priced at the top of its historical P/E range (22.0x) while fundamentals are deteriorating—YoY EPS down 10.1% and revenue down 3.0%. The risk/reward is roughly balanced: the quality business offsets stretched valuation, but there is no clear margin of safety.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Home Depot is a category-dominant franchise with strong strategic execution, but the stock is priced at the top of its historical P/E range (22.0x) while fundamentals are deteriorating—YoY EPS down 10.1% and revenue down 3.0%. The risk/reward is roughly balanced: the quality business offsets stretched valuation, but there is no clear margin of safety.
key_factors: P/E of 22.0x sits at the top of its 365-day range (16.8–22.5), leaving little room for multiple expansion and exposing the stock to mean-reversion risk.
Q3 2023 EPS declined 10.1% YoY to $3.81 and revenue fell 3.0% YoY to $37.71B, reflecting a fundamental deceleration that the elevated valuation does not fully discount.
Balance sheet leverage is extreme with liabilities-to-equity of 51.9x ($74.15B/$1.43B), providing minimal equity cushion against operational or macroeconomic shocks.

Strategic execution remains solid—digital sales grew 5% YoY in Q3 2023 and the interconnected retail and Pro customer strategies are driving results, supporting the franchise's long-term competitive position.

Item 1. Business
General Development of Business
Summary Description of Chevron
Chevron Corporation,* a Delaware corporation, manages its investments in subsidiaries and affiliates and provides administrative, financial, management and technology support to U.S. and international subsidiaries that engage in integrated energy and chemicals operations. Upstream operations consist primarily of exploring for, developing, producing and transporting crude oil and natural gas; processing, liquefaction, transportation and regasification associated with liquefied natural gas; transporting crude oil by major international oil export pipelines; transporting, storage and marketing of natural gas; and a gas-to-liquids plant. Downstream operations consist primarily of refining crude oil into petroleum products; marketing of crude oil, refined products, and lubricants; manufacturing and marketing of renewable fuels; transporting crude oil and refined products by pipeline, marine vessel, motor equipment and rail car; and manufacturing and marketing of commodity petrochemicals, plastics for industrial uses and fuel and lubricant additives.
A list of the company's significant subsidiaries is presented in Exhibit 21.1.
Overview of Petroleum Industry
Petroleum industry operations and profitability are influenced by many factors. Prices for crude oil, natural gas, liquefied natural gas, petroleum products and petrochemicals are generally determined by supply and demand. Production levels from the members of Organization of Petroleum Exporting Countries (OPEC), Russia and the United States are the major factors in determining worldwide supply. Demand for crude oil and its products and for natural gas is largely driven by the conditions of local, national and global economies, although weather patterns, the pace of energy transition and taxation relative to other energy sources also play a significant part. Laws and governmental policies, particularly in the areas of taxation, energy and the environment, affect where and how companies invest, conduct their operations, select feedstocks, and formulate their products and, in some cases, limit their profits directly.
